verses.latin_vulgate: Elevans autem Jacob oculos suos, vidit venientem Esau, et cum eo quadringentos viros : divisitque filios Liæ et Rachel, ambarumque famularum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֨א יַעֲקֹ֜ב עֵינָ֗יו וַיַּרְא֙ וְהִנֵּ֣ה עֵשָׂ֣ו בָּ֔א וְעִמּ֕וֹ אַרְבַּ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת אִ֑ישׁ וַיַּ֣חַץ אֶת־הַיְלָדִ֗ים עַל־לֵאָה֙ וְעַל־רָחֵ֔ל וְעַ֖ל שְׁתֵּ֥י הַשְּׁפָחֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob lifting up his eyes, saw Esau coming, and with him four hundred men: and he divided the children of Lia and of Rachel, and of the two handmaids.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob nosti silmänsä ja näki, ja katso, Esau tuli, ja neljäsataa miestä hänen kanssansa: ja hän jakoi lapset Lealle ja Rakelille, ja molemmille piioille.
verses.king_james: And Jacob lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, Esau came, and with him four hundred men. And he divided the children unto Leah, and unto Rachel, and unto the two handmaids.

verses.latin_vulgate: et posuit utramque ancillam, et liberos earum, in principio : Liam vero, et filios ejus, in secundo loco : Rachel autem et Joseph novissimos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֧שֶׂם אֶת־הַשְּׁפָח֛וֹת וְאֶת־יַלְדֵיהֶ֖ן רִֽאשֹׁנָ֑ה וְאֶת־לֵאָ֤ה וִֽילָדֶ֙יהָ֙ אַחֲרֹנִ֔ים וְאֶת־רָחֵ֥ל וְאֶת־יוֹסֵ֖ף אַחֲרֹנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he put both the handmaids and their children foremost: and Lia and her children in the second place: and Rachel and Joseph last.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja asetti piiat lapsinensa ensimmäiseksi; sitälähin Lean hänen lastensa kanssa, ja Rakelin Josephin kanssa jälimmäiseksi.
verses.king_james: And he put the handmaids and their children foremost, and Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph hindermost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipse progrediens adoravit pronus in terram septies, donec appropinquaret frater ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְה֖וּא עָבַ֣ר לִפְנֵיהֶ֑ם וַיִּשְׁתַּ֤חוּ אַ֙רְצָה֙ שֶׁ֣בַע פְּעָמִ֔ים עַד־גִּשְׁתּ֖וֹ עַד־אָחִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he went forward and bowed down with his face to the ground seven times, until his brother came near.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta itse hän kävi heidän edellänsä: ja kumarsi maahan seitsemän kertaa, siihenasti kuin hän veljeänsä lähestyi.
verses.king_james: And he passed over before them, and bowed himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Currens itaque Esau obviam fratri suo, amplexatus est eum : stringensque collum ejus, et osculans flevit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֨רׇץ עֵשָׂ֤ו לִקְרָאתוֹ֙ וַֽיְחַבְּקֵ֔הוּ וַיִּפֹּ֥ל עַל־צַוָּארָ֖ו וַׄיִּׄשָּׁׄקֵ֑ׄהׄוּׄ וַיִּבְכּֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Esau ran to meet his brother, and embraced him: and clasping him fast about the neck, and kissing him, wept.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Esau juoksi häntä vastaan, ja syliinsä otti hänen, ja halasi häntä kaulasta, ja suuta antoi hänen: ja he itkivät.
verses.king_james: And Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck, and kissed him: and they wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levatisque oculis, vidit mulieres et parvulos earum, et ait : Quid sibi volunt isti ? et si ad te pertinent ? Respondit : Parvuli sunt quos donavit mihi Deus servo tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֣א אֶת־עֵינָ֗יו וַיַּ֤רְא אֶת־הַנָּשִׁים֙ וְאֶת־הַיְלָדִ֔ים וַיֹּ֖אמֶר מִי־אֵ֣לֶּה לָּ֑ךְ וַיֹּאמַ֕ר הַיְלָדִ֕ים אֲשֶׁר־חָנַ֥ן אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת־עַבְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And lifting up his eyes, he saw the women and their children, and said: What mean these? And do they belong to thee? He answered: They are the children which God hath given to me, thy servant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän nosti silmänsä, ja näki vaimot ja lapset, ja sanoi: kutka nämät kanssas ovat? Hän vastasi: ne ovat lapset, jotka Jumala on lahjoittanut sinun palvelialles.
verses.king_james: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children; and said, Who are those with thee? And he said, The children which God hath graciously given thy servant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et appropinquantes ancillæ et filii earum, incurvati sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּגַּ֧שְׁןָ הַשְּׁפָח֛וֹת הֵ֥נָּה וְיַלְדֵיהֶ֖ן וַתִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶֽיןָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the handmaids and their children came near and bowed themselves.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin piiat lähestyivät lapsinensa ja kumarsivat hänen edessänsä.
verses.king_james: Then the handmaidens came near, they and their children, and they bowed themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accessit quoque Lia cum pueris suis : et cum similiter adorassent, extremi Joseph et Rachel adoraverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּגַּ֧שׁ גַּם־לֵאָ֛ה וִילָדֶ֖יהָ וַיִּֽשְׁתַּחֲו֑וּ וְאַחַ֗ר נִגַּ֥שׁ יוֹסֵ֛ף וְרָחֵ֖ל וַיִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lia also, with her children, came near and bowed down in like manner; and last of all, Joseph and Rachel bowed down.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lähestyi myös Lea lapsinensa, ja kumarsivat hänen edessänsä. Sitte tuli Joseph ja Rakel, jotka myös kumarsivat hänen edessänsä.
verses.king_james: And Leah also with her children came near, and bowed themselves: and after came Joseph near and Rachel, and they bowed themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Esau : Quænam sunt istæ turmæ quas obviam habui ? Respondit : Ut invenirem gratiam coram domino meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מִ֥י לְךָ֛ כׇּל־הַמַּחֲנֶ֥ה הַזֶּ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר פָּגָ֑שְׁתִּי וַיֹּ֕אמֶר לִמְצֹא־חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Esau said: What are the droves that I met? He answered: That I might find favour before my lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: mitäs tahdot kaiken sen joukon kanssa, kuin minä kohdannut olen? Hän vastasi: löytääkseni armoa minun herrani edessä.
verses.king_james: And he said, What meanest thou by all this drove which I met? And he said, These are to find grace in the sight of my lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille ait : Habeo plurima, frater mi, sint tua tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר עֵשָׂ֖ו יֶשׁ־לִ֣י רָ֑ב אָחִ֕י יְהִ֥י לְךָ֖ אֲשֶׁר־לָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he said: I have plenty, my brother, keep what is thine for thyself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Esau sanoi: minulla on kyllä, minun veljeni, pidä omas.
verses.king_james: And Esau said, I have enough, my brother; keep that thou hast unto thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Jacob : Noli ita, obsecro : sed si inveni gratiam in oculis tuis, accipe munusculum de manibus meis. Sic enim vidi faciem tuam, quasi viderim vultum Dei : esto mihi propitius,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֗ב אַל־נָא֙ אִם־נָ֨א מָצָ֤אתִי חֵן֙ בְּעֵינֶ֔יךָ וְלָקַחְתָּ֥ מִנְחָתִ֖י מִיָּדִ֑י כִּ֣י עַל־כֵּ֞ן רָאִ֣יתִי פָנֶ֗יךָ כִּרְאֹ֛ת פְּנֵ֥י אֱלֹהִ֖ים וַתִּרְצֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob said: Do not so I beseech thee, but if I have found favour in thy eyes, receive a little present at my hands: for I have seen thy face, as if I should have seen the countenance of God: be gracious to me,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jakob vastasi: ei niin, mutta jos minä olen löytänyt armon sinun edessäs, niin ota minun lahjani minun kädestäni: sillä minä näin sinun kasvos, niinkuin minä olisin nähnyt Jumalan kasvot; ettäs olet minut hyväksi ottanut.
verses.king_james: And Jacob said, Nay, I pray thee, if now I have found grace in thy sight, then receive my present at my hand: for therefore I have seen thy face, as though I had seen the face of God, and thou wast pleased with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: et suscipe benedictionem quam attuli tibi, et quam donavit mihi Deus tribuens omnia. Vix fratre compellente, suscipiens,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַח־נָ֤א אֶת־בִּרְכָתִי֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הֻבָ֣את לָ֔ךְ כִּֽי־חַנַּ֥נִי אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְכִ֣י יֶשׁ־לִי־כֹ֑ל וַיִּפְצַר־בּ֖וֹ וַיִּקָּֽח׃
verses.douay_rheims: And take the blessing which I have brought thee, and which God hath given me, who giveth all things. He took it with much ado at his brother's earnest pressing him,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ota siis se siunaus, jonka minä olen tuonut sinulle: sillä Jumala on sen minulle antanut, ja minulla on kyllä kaikkinaista. Niin hän vaati häntä ottamaan.
verses.king_james: Take, I pray thee, my blessing that is brought to thee; because God hath dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough. And he urged him, and he took it.

verses.latin_vulgate: ait : Gradiamur simul, eroque socius itineris tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר נִסְעָ֣ה וְנֵלֵ֑כָה וְאֵלְכָ֖ה לְנֶגְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said: Let us go on together, and I will accompany thee in thy journey.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja (Esau) sanoi: lähtekäämme jo matkustamaan, minä vaellan sinun kanssas.
verses.king_james: And he said, Let us take our journey, and let us go, and I will go before thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Jacob : Nosti, domine mi, quod parvulos habeam teneros, et oves, et boves fœtas mecum : quas si plus in ambulando fecero laborare, morientur una die cuncti greges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֗יו אֲדֹנִ֤י יֹדֵ֙עַ֙ כִּֽי־הַיְלָדִ֣ים רַכִּ֔ים וְהַצֹּ֥אן וְהַבָּקָ֖ר עָל֣וֹת עָלָ֑י וּדְפָקוּם֙ י֣וֹם אֶחָ֔ד וָמֵ֖תוּ כׇּל־הַצֹּֽאן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob said: My lord, thou knowest that I have with me tender children, and sheep, and kine with young: which if I should cause to be overdriven, in one day all the flocks will die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän sanoi hänelle: minun herrani tietää minulla olevan pieniä lapsia, ja imettäväisiä lampaita ja karjaa: jos ne yhtenä päivänä äkisti ajettaisiin, niin kuolis lauma.
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, My lord knoweth that the children are tender, and the flocks and herds with young are with me: and if men should overdrive them one day, all the flock will die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præcedat dominus meus ante servum suum : et ego sequar paulatim vestigia ejus, sicut videro parvulos meos posse, donec veniam ad dominum meum in Seir.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַעֲבׇר־נָ֥א אֲדֹנִ֖י לִפְנֵ֣י עַבְדּ֑וֹ וַאֲנִ֞י אֶֽתְנָהֲלָ֣ה לְאִטִּ֗י לְרֶ֨גֶל הַמְּלָאכָ֤ה אֲשֶׁר־לְפָנַי֙ וּלְרֶ֣גֶל הַיְלָדִ֔ים עַ֛ד אֲשֶׁר־אָבֹ֥א אֶל־אֲדֹנִ֖י שֵׂעִֽירָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: May it please my lord to go before his servant: and I will follow softly after him, as I shall see my children to be able, until I come to my lord in Seir.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin menkään minun herrani palveliansa edellä: mutta minä seuraan hiljaksensa senjälkeen kuin karja ja lapset käydä voivat, siihenasti kuin minä tulen minun herrani tykö Seiriin.
verses.king_james: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant: and I will lead on softly, according as the cattle that goeth before me and the children be able to endure, until I come unto my lord unto Seir.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Esau : Oro te, ut de populo qui mecum est, saltem socii remaneant viæ tuæ. Non est, inquit, necesse : hoc uno tantum indigeo, ut inveniam gratiam in conspectu tuo, domine mi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר עֵשָׂ֔ו אַצִּֽיגָה־נָּ֣א עִמְּךָ֔ מִן־הָעָ֖ם אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתִּ֑י וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ לָ֣מָּה זֶּ֔ה אֶמְצָא־חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Esau answered: I beseech thee, that some of the people, at least, who are with me, may stay to accompany thee in the way. And he said: There is no necessity: I want nothing else but only to find favour, my lord, in thy sight.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Esau sanoi: minä jätän kuitenkin sinun tykös jotakin siitä väestä, kuin minun kanssani on. Hän vastasi: mihinkä se? Anna minun ainoastansa löytää armo minun herrani edessä.
verses.king_james: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee some of the folk that are with me. And he said, What needeth it? let me find grace in the sight of my lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversus est itaque illo die Esau itinere quo venerat in Seir.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֩שׇׁב֩ בַּיּ֨וֹם הַה֥וּא עֵשָׂ֛ו לְדַרְכּ֖וֹ שֵׂעִֽירָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Esau returned that day, the way that he came, to Seir.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Esau palasi sinä päivänä tietänsä myöten Seiriin.
verses.king_james: So Esau returned that day on his way unto Seir.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Jacob venit in Socoth : ubi ædificata domo et fixis tentoriis appellavit nomen loci illius Socoth, id est, Tabernacula.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיַעֲקֹב֙ נָסַ֣ע סֻכֹּ֔תָה וַיִּ֥בֶן ל֖וֹ בָּ֑יִת וּלְמִקְנֵ֙הוּ֙ עָשָׂ֣ה סֻכֹּ֔ת עַל־כֵּ֛ן קָרָ֥א שֵׁם־הַמָּק֖וֹם סֻכּֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob came to Socoth: where having built a house, and pitched tents, he called the name of the place Socoth, that is, Tents.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jakob matkusti Sukkotiin, ja rakensi itsellensä huoneen, ja teki majat karjallensa. Sentähden kutsui hän sen paikan Sukkot.
verses.king_james: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built him an house, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transivitque in Salem urbem Sichimorum, quæ est in terra Chanaan, postquam reversus est de Mesopotamia Syriæ : et habitavit juxta oppidum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֩ יַעֲקֹ֨ב שָׁלֵ֜ם עִ֣יר שְׁכֶ֗ם אֲשֶׁר֙ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֔עַן בְּבֹא֖וֹ מִפַּדַּ֣ן אֲרָ֑ם וַיִּ֖חַן אֶת־פְּנֵ֥י הָעִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he passed over to Salem, a city of the Sichemites, which is in the land of Chanaan, after he returned from Mesopotamia of Syria: and he dwelt by the town.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte tuli Jakob rauhoitettuna siihen kaupunkiin Sikem, joka on Kanaanin maalla, sitte kuin hän oli tullut Mesopotamiasta; ja sioitti itsensä kaupungin kohdalle.
verses.king_james: And Jacob came to Shalem, a city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan–aram; and pitched his tent before the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Emitque partem agri, in qua fixerat tabernacula, a filiis Hemor patris Sichem centum agnis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֜קֶן אֶת־חֶלְקַ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֤ר נָֽטָה־שָׁם֙ אׇהֳל֔וֹ מִיַּ֥ד בְּנֵֽי־חֲמ֖וֹר אֲבִ֣י שְׁכֶ֑ם בְּמֵאָ֖ה קְשִׂיטָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he bought that part of the field, in which he pitched his tents, of the children of Hemor, the father of Sichem, for a hundred lambs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja osti murun peltoa, johonka hän teki majansa, Hemorin, Sikemin isän lapsilta, sadan penningin edestä.
verses.king_james: And he bought a parcel of a field, where he had spread his tent, at the hand of the children of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for an hundred pieces of money.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erecto ibi altari, invocavit super illud fortissimum Deum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּצֶּב־שָׁ֖ם מִזְבֵּ֑חַ וַיִּ֨קְרָא־ל֔וֹ אֵ֖ל אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And raising an altar there, he invoked upon it the most mighty God of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja rakensi siinä alttarin: ja rukoili väkevää Israelin Jumalan nimeä.
verses.king_james: And he erected there an altar, and called it El–elohe–Israel.
